# servicenow_transform_entry (Resource)

`servicenow_transform_entry` manages an individual field mapping (sys_transform_entry) inside a transform map.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages a field mapping (sys_transform_entry) inside a transform map.
resource "servicenow_transform_map" "parent" {
  name         = "Example User Import"
  source_table = "u_user_import"
  target_table = "sys_user"
}

resource "servicenow_transform_entry" "user_name" {
  map          = servicenow_transform_map.parent.id
  source_field = "u_email"
  target_field = "user_name"
  coalesce     = true
  order        = 100
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `map` (String) sys_id of the parent transform map this entry belongs to.
- `target_field` (String) Target field name on the target table.

### Optional

- `coalesce` (Boolean) If true, use this field to match existing records during transformation.
- `protection_policy` (String) Determines how application files are protected when downloaded or installed. Can be empty for no protection, 'read' for read-only protection or 'protected'.
- `referenced_value_field_name` (String) Name of the field on the reference table to match against (maps to ServiceNow's `reference_value_field` column).
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `source_field` (String) Source field name on the source table.
- `source_script` (String) Optional source script to compute the value when use_source_script is true. ServiceNow seeds a default function body when the entry is created.
- `use_source_script` (Boolean) If true, use the source script instead of the source field for the value.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_transform_entry.example <sys_id>
```
